sn34.13:1.4.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ samāpattikusalo hoti, na samādhismiṁ kallitakusalo.
sn34.13:1.4.eng: One meditator is skilled in entering immersion but not in positivity for immersion. …”

sn34.13:1.5.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ kallitakusalo hoti, na samādhismiṁ samāpattikusalo.

sn34.13:1.6.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī neva samādhismiṁ samāpattikusalo hoti, na ca samādhismiṁ kallitakusalo.

sn34.13:1.7.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ samāpattikusalo ca hoti, samādhismiṁ kallitakusalo ca.

sn34.14:1.4.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ samāpattikusalo hoti, na samādhismiṁ ārammaṇakusalo.
sn34.14:1.4.eng: One meditator is skilled in entering immersion but not in the supports for it. …”

sn34.14:1.5.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ ārammaṇakusalo hoti, na samādhismiṁ samāpattikusalo.

sn34.14:1.6.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī neva samādhismiṁ samāpattikusalo hoti, na ca samādhismiṁ ārammaṇakusalo.

sn34.14:1.7.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ samāpattikusalo ca hoti, samādhismiṁ ārammaṇakusalo ca.

sn34.15:1.4.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ samāpattikusalo hoti, na samādhismiṁ gocarakusalo.
sn34.15:1.4.eng: One meditator is skilled in entering immersion but not in the territory of immersion. …”

sn34.15:1.5.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ gocarakusalo hoti, na samādhismiṁ samāpattikusalo.

sn34.15:1.6.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī neva samādhismiṁ samāpattikusalo hoti, na ca samādhismiṁ gocarakusalo.

sn34.15:1.7.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ samāpattikusalo ca hoti, samādhismiṁ gocarakusalo ca.

sn34.16:1.4.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ samāpattikusalo hoti, na samādhismiṁ abhinīhārakusalo.
sn34.16:1.4.eng: One meditator is skilled in entering immersion but not in projecting the mind purified by immersion. …”

sn34.16:1.5.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ abhinīhārakusalo hoti, na samādhismiṁ samāpattikusalo.

sn34.16:1.6.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī neva samādhismiṁ samāpattikusalo hoti, na ca samādhismiṁ abhinīhārakusalo.

sn34.16:1.7.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ samāpattikusalo ca hoti, samādhismiṁ abhinīhārakusalo ca.

sn34.17:1.4.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ samāpattikusalo hoti, na samādhismiṁ sakkaccakārī.
sn34.17:1.4.eng: One meditator is skilled in entering immersion but not in practicing carefully for it. …”

sn34.17:1.5.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ sakkaccakārī hoti, na samādhismiṁ samāpattikusalo.

sn34.17:1.6.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī neva samādhismiṁ samāpattikusalo hoti, na ca samādhismiṁ sakkaccakārī.

sn34.17:1.7.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ samāpattikusalo ca hoti, samādhismiṁ sakkaccakārī ca.

sn34.18:1.4.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ samāpattikusalo hoti, na samādhismiṁ sātaccakārī.
sn34.18:1.4.eng: One meditator is skilled in entering immersion but not in practicing persistently for it. …”

sn34.18:1.5.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ sātaccakārī hoti, na samādhismiṁ samāpattikusalo.

sn34.18:1.6.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī neva samādhismiṁ samāpattikusalo hoti, na ca samādhismiṁ sātaccakārī.

sn34.18:1.7.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ samāpattikusalo ca hoti, samādhismiṁ sātaccakārī ca.

sn34.19:1.4.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ samāpattikusalo hoti, na samādhismiṁ sappāyakārī.
sn34.19:1.4.eng: One meditator is skilled in entering immersion but not in doing what’s conducive to it. …”

sn34.19:1.5.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ sappāyakārī hoti, na samādhismiṁ samāpattikusalo.

sn34.19:1.6.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī neva samādhismiṁ samāpattikusalo hoti, na ca samādhismiṁ sappāyakārī.

sn34.19:1.7.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ samāpattikusalo ca hoti, samādhismiṁ sappāyakārī ca.

sn34.19:1.8.pli: Tatra, bhikkhave, yvāyaṁ jhāyī samādhismiṁ samāpattikusalo ca hoti samādhismiṁ sappāyakārī ca ayaṁ imesaṁ catunnaṁ jhāyīnaṁ aggo ca seṭṭho ca mokkho ca uttamo ca pavaro ca.

sn34.19:1.9.pli: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, gavā khīraṁ, khīramhā dadhi, dadhimhā navanītaṁ, navanītamhā sappi, sappimhā sappimaṇḍo tatra aggamakkhāyati;

sn34.19:1.10.pli: evameva kho, bhikkhave, yvāyaṁ jhāyī samādhismiṁ samāpattikusalo ca hoti samādhismiṁ sappāyakārī ca ayaṁ imesaṁ catunnaṁ jhāyīnaṁ aggo ca seṭṭho ca mokkho ca uttamo ca pavaro cā”ti.

sn34.2:1.4.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo hoti, na samādhismiṁ ṭhitikusalo.
sn34.2:1.4.eng: One meditator is skilled in immersion but not in remaining in it.
sn34.2:1.4.comm: They cannot stay long, or determine the length of the absorbed state.

sn34.2:1.5.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ ṭhitikusalo hoti, na samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo.
sn34.2:1.5.eng: One meditator is skilled in remaining in immersion but is not skilled in immersion.

sn34.2:1.6.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī neva samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo hoti, na ca samādhismiṁ ṭhitikusalo.
sn34.2:1.6.eng: One meditator is skilled neither in immersion nor in remaining in it.

sn34.2:1.7.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo ca hoti, samādhismiṁ ṭhitikusalo ca.
sn34.2:1.7.eng: One meditator is skilled both in immersion and in remaining in it.

sn34.2:1.8.pli: Tatra, bhikkhave, yvāyaṁ jhāyī samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo ca hoti samādhismiṁ ṭhitikusalo ca ayaṁ imesaṁ catunnaṁ jhāyīnaṁ aggo ca seṭṭho ca mokkho ca uttamo ca pavaro ca.
sn34.2:1.8.eng: Of these, the meditator skilled in immersion and in remaining in it is the foremost, best, leading, highest, and finest of the four.

sn34.2:1.9.pli: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, gavā khīraṁ, khīramhā dadhi, dadhimhā navanītaṁ, navanītamhā sappi, sappimhā sappimaṇḍo tatra aggamakkhāyati;
sn34.2:1.9.eng: From a cow comes milk, from milk comes curds, from curds come butter, from butter comes ghee, and from ghee comes cream of ghee. And the cream of ghee is said to be the best of these.

sn34.2:1.10.pli: evameva kho, bhikkhave, yvāyaṁ jhāyī samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo ca hoti samādhismiṁ ṭhitikusalo ca ayaṁ imesaṁ catunnaṁ jhāyīnaṁ aggo ca seṭṭho ca mokkho ca uttamo ca pavaro cā”ti.
sn34.2:1.10.eng: In the same way, the meditator skilled in immersion and remaining in it is the foremost, best, leading, highest, and finest of the four.”

sn34.20-27:0.3.eng: Eight on Remaining and Emergence, Etc.

sn34.20-27:1.4.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ ṭhitikusalo hoti, na samādhismiṁ vuṭṭhānakusalo.
sn34.20-27:1.4.eng: One meditator is skilled in remaining in immersion but not in emerging from it. …”

sn34.20-27:1.5.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ vuṭṭhānakusalo hoti, na samādhismiṁ ṭhitikusalo.

sn34.20-27:1.6.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī neva samādhismiṁ ṭhitikusalo hoti, na ca samādhismiṁ vuṭṭhānakusalo.

sn34.20-27:1.7.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ ṭhitikusalo ca hoti, samādhismiṁ vuṭṭhānakusalo ca.

sn34.20-27:1.11.pli: (Purimamūlakāni viya yāva sattavīsatimā ṭhitimūlakasappāyakārīsuttā aṭṭha suttāni pūretabbāni.)
sn34.20-27:1.11.eng: (These eight discourses should be told in full as the previous set.)

sn34.28-34:0.3.eng: Seven on Emergence and Gladdening, Etc.

sn34.28-34:1.4.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ vuṭṭhānakusalo hoti, na samādhismiṁ kallitakusalo …
sn34.28-34:1.4.eng: One meditator is skilled in emerging from immersion but not in positivity for immersion. …”

sn34.28-34:1.5.pli: samādhismiṁ kallitakusalo hoti, na samādhismiṁ vuṭṭhānakusalo …

sn34.28-34:1.6.pli: neva samādhismiṁ vuṭṭhānakusalo hoti, na ca samādhismiṁ kallitakusalo …

sn34.28-34:1.7.pli: samādhismiṁ vuṭṭhānakusalo ca hoti samādhismiṁ kallitakusalo ca.

sn34.28-34:1.11.pli: (Purimamūlakāni viya yāva catuttiṁsatimā vuṭṭhānamūlakasappāyakārīsuttā satta suttāni pūretabbāni.)
sn34.28-34:1.11.eng: (These seven discourses should be told in full as the previous set.)

sn34.3:1.4.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo hoti, na samādhismiṁ vuṭṭhānakusalo.
sn34.3:1.4.eng: One meditator is skilled in immersion but not in emerging from it. …”
sn34.3:1.4.comm: They cannot determine the time for emerging from the meditation.

sn34.3:1.5.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ vuṭṭhānakusalo hoti, na samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo.

sn34.3:1.6.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī neva samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo hoti, na ca samādhismiṁ vuṭṭhānakusalo.

sn34.3:1.7.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo ca hoti, samādhismiṁ vuṭṭhānakusalo ca.

sn34.3:1.8.pli: Tatra, bhikkhave, yvāyaṁ jhāyī samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo ca hoti samādhismiṁ vuṭṭhānakusalo ca ayaṁ imesaṁ catunnaṁ jhāyīnaṁ aggo ca seṭṭho ca mokkho ca uttamo ca pavaro ca.

sn34.35-40:0.3.eng: Six on Gladdening and Support, Etc.

sn34.35-40:1.2.eng: “One meditator is skilled in positivity for immersion but not in the supports for immersion. …”

sn34.35-40:1.3.pli: samādhismiṁ ārammaṇakusalo hoti, na samādhismiṁ kallitakusalo …

sn34.35-40:1.4.pli: neva samādhismiṁ kallitakusalo hoti, na ca samādhismiṁ ārammaṇakusalo …

sn34.35-40:1.5.pli: samādhismiṁ kallitakusalo ca hoti, samādhismiṁ ārammaṇakusalo ca.

sn34.35-40:1.9.pli: (Purimamūlakāni viya yāva cattālīsamā kallitamūlakasappāyakārīsuttā cha suttāni pūretabbāni.)
sn34.35-40:1.9.eng: (These six discourses should be told in full as the previous set.)

sn34.4:1.4.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo hoti, na samādhismiṁ kallitakusalo.
sn34.4:1.4.eng: One meditator is skilled in immersion but not in positivity for immersion. …”
sn34.4:1.4.comm: Kallita means “healthy and happy, well-disposed”, and is explained by the commentary as “gladdening the mind”. Any meditator knows that there are ups and downs in the process, and it is essential to keep the mind balanced, with a positive attitude.

sn34.4:1.5.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ kallitakusalo hoti, na samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo.

sn34.4:1.6.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī neva samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo hoti, na ca samādhismiṁ kallitakusalo.

sn34.4:1.7.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo ca hoti, samādhismiṁ kallitakusalo ca.

sn34.4:1.8.pli: Tatra, bhikkhave, yvāyaṁ jhāyī samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo ca hoti samādhismiṁ kallitakusalo ca ayaṁ imesaṁ catunnaṁ jhāyīnaṁ aggo ca seṭṭho ca mokkho ca uttamo ca pavaro ca.

sn34.41-45:0.3.eng: Five on Support and Territory, Etc.

sn34.41-45:1.2.eng: “One meditator is skilled in the supports for immersion but not in the territory of immersion. …”

sn34.41-45:1.3.pli: samādhismiṁ gocarakusalo hoti, na samādhismiṁ ārammaṇakusalo …

sn34.41-45:1.4.pli: neva samādhismiṁ ārammaṇakusalo hoti, na ca samādhismiṁ gocarakusalo …

sn34.41-45:1.5.pli: samādhismiṁ ārammaṇakusalo ca hoti, samādhismiṁ gocarakusalo ca.

sn34.41-45:1.9.pli: (Purimamūlakāni viya yāva pañcacattālīsamā ārammaṇamūlakasappāyakārīsuttā pañca suttāni pūretabbāni.)
sn34.41-45:1.9.eng: (These five discourses should be told in full as the previous set.)

sn34.46:0.3.eng: Four on Territory and Projection, Etc.

sn34.46:1.2.eng: “One meditator is skilled in the territory of immersion but not in projecting the mind purified by immersion. …”

sn34.46:1.3.pli: samādhismiṁ abhinīhārakusalo hoti, na samādhismiṁ gocarakusalo …

sn34.46:1.4.pli: neva samādhismiṁ gocarakusalo hoti, na ca samādhismiṁ abhinīhārakusalo …

sn34.46:1.5.pli: samādhismiṁ gocarakusalo ca hoti, samādhismiṁ abhinīhārakusalo ca …

sn34.46:1.6.pli: seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, gavā khīraṁ, khīramhā dadhi, dadhimhā navanītaṁ, navanītamhā sappi, sappimhā sappimaṇḍo tatra aggamakkhāyati;

sn34.46:1.7.pli: evameva kho, bhikkhave, yvāyaṁ jhāyī samādhismiṁ gocarakusalo ca hoti samādhismiṁ abhinīhārakusalo ca ayaṁ imesaṁ catunnaṁ jhāyīnaṁ …pe…

sn34.47:1.1.eng: “One meditator is skilled in the territory of immersion but not in practicing carefully for it. …”

sn34.47:1.2.eng: (These four discourses should be told in full as the previous set.)

sn34.48:1.1.eng: “One meditator is skilled in the territory of immersion but not in practicing persistently for it. …”

sn34.49:1.1.eng: “One meditator is skilled in the territory of immersion but not in doing what’s conducive to it. …”

sn34.5:1.4.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo hoti, na samādhismiṁ ārammaṇakusalo.
sn34.5:1.4.eng: One meditator is skilled in immersion but not in the supports for immersion. …”
sn34.5:1.4.comm: “Support” is ārammaṇa, namely the different subjects of meditation. This is the only term in this list that does not appear elsewhere in the early canon. It does not mean “object”, which is exclusively a later sense, derived from the (again later) use of the kasiṇa as a physical “object” or “disk” on which to focus attention (for the Sutta usage, see MN 77:24.11). An “object” is something that has an “objective” reality independent of the observer. But for the Suttas, meditations like the breath, or love, or an inner reflection on mortality are dimensions of living experience that change and grow through and with the course of meditation. The meditation subject is not something at which the mind is directed, it is something with which the mind grows.

sn34.5:1.5.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ ārammaṇakusalo hoti, na samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo.

sn34.5:1.6.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī neva samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo hoti, na ca samādhismiṁ ārammaṇakusalo.

sn34.5:1.7.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo ca hoti, samādhismiṁ ārammaṇakusalo ca.

sn34.5:1.8.pli: Tatra, bhikkhave, yvāyaṁ jhāyī samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo ca hoti samādhismiṁ ārammaṇakusalo ca ayaṁ imesaṁ catunnaṁ jhāyīnaṁ aggo ca seṭṭho ca mokkho ca uttamo ca pavaro ca.

sn34.50:1.2.eng: “One meditator is skilled in projecting the mind purified by immersion but not in practicing carefully for it. …”

sn34.50:1.3.pli: samādhismiṁ sakkaccakārī hoti, na samādhismiṁ abhinīhārakusalo …

sn34.50:1.4.pli: neva samādhismiṁ abhinīhārakusalo hoti, na ca samādhismiṁ sakkaccakārī …

sn34.50:1.5.pli: samādhismiṁ abhinīhārakusalo ca hoti, samādhismiṁ sakkaccakārī ca.

sn34.51:1.1.eng: “One meditator is skilled in projecting the mind purified by immersion but not in practicing persistently for it. …”

sn34.52:1.1.eng: “One meditator is skilled in projecting the mind purified by immersion but not in doing what’s conducive to it. …”

sn34.53:1.2.eng: “One meditator is skilled in practicing carefully for immersion but not in practicing persistently for it. …”

sn34.53:1.3.pli: samādhismiṁ sātaccakārī hoti, na samādhismiṁ sakkaccakārī …

sn34.53:1.4.pli: neva samādhismiṁ sakkaccakārī hoti, na ca samādhismiṁ sātaccakārī …

sn34.53:1.5.pli: samādhismiṁ sakkaccakārī ca hoti, samādhismiṁ sātaccakārī ca.

sn34.54:1.1.eng: “One meditator is skilled in practicing carefully for immersion but not in doing what’s conducive to it. …”

sn34.55:1.4.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ sātaccakārī hoti, na samādhismiṁ sappāyakārī.
sn34.55:1.4.eng: One meditator is skilled in practicing persistently for immersion but not in doing what’s conducive to it.

sn34.55:1.5.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ sappāyakārī hoti, na samādhismiṁ sātaccakārī.
sn34.55:1.5.eng: One meditator is skilled in doing what’s conducive to immersion but not in practicing persistently for it.

sn34.55:1.6.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī neva samādhismiṁ sātaccakārī hoti, na ca samādhismiṁ sappāyakārī.
sn34.55:1.6.eng: One meditator is skilled neither in practicing persistently for immersion nor in doing what’s conducive to it.

sn34.55:1.7.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ sātaccakārī ca hoti, samādhismiṁ sappāyakārī ca.
sn34.55:1.7.eng: One meditator is skilled both in practicing persistently for immersion and in doing what’s conducive to it.

sn34.55:1.8.pli: Tatra, bhikkhave, yvāyaṁ jhāyī samādhismiṁ sātaccakārī ca hoti samādhismiṁ sappāyakārī ca ayaṁ imesaṁ catunnaṁ jhāyīnaṁ aggo ca seṭṭho ca mokkho ca uttamo ca pavaro ca.
sn34.55:1.8.eng: Of these, the meditator skilled both in practicing persistently for immersion and in doing what’s conducive to it is the foremost, best, leading, highest, and finest of the four.

sn34.55:1.9.pli: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, gavā khīraṁ, khīramhā dadhi, dadhimhā navanītaṁ, navanītamhā sappi, sappimhā sappimaṇḍo tatra aggamakkhāyati;
sn34.55:1.9.eng: From a cow comes milk, from milk comes curds, from curds come butter, from butter comes ghee, and from ghee comes cream of ghee. And the cream of ghee is said to be the best of these.

sn34.55:1.10.pli: evameva kho, bhikkhave, yvāyaṁ jhāyī samādhismiṁ sātaccakārī ca hoti, samādhismiṁ sappāyakārī ca ayaṁ imesaṁ catunnaṁ jhāyīnaṁ aggo ca seṭṭho ca mokkho ca uttamo ca pavaro cā”ti.
sn34.55:1.10.eng: In the same way, the meditator skilled both in practicing persistently for immersion and in doing what’s conducive to it is the foremost, best, leading, highest, and finest of the four.”

sn34.55:1.11.eng: That is what the Buddha said.

sn34.55:1.12.pli: Attamanā te bhikkhū bhagavato bhāsitaṁ abhinandunti.
sn34.55:1.12.eng: Satisfied, the mendicants approved what the Buddha said.

sn34.55:1.14.eng: (All should be told in full as per the fifty-fifth discourse.)

sn34.55:2.5.eng: The Linked Discourses on Absorption are complete.

sn34.55:3.7.eng: The Book of the Aggregates is finished.

sn34.6:1.4.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo hoti, na samādhismiṁ gocarakusalo.
sn34.6:1.4.eng: One meditator is skilled in immersion but not in the territory of immersion. …”
sn34.6:1.4.comm: A gocara (“territory”) is literally the “cowpasture” and figuratively the “proper resort” or “territory”. At SN 47.6, a meditator’s “territory” is said to be the four kinds of mindfulness meditation.

sn34.6:1.5.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ gocarakusalo hoti, na samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo.

sn34.6:1.6.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī neva samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo hoti, na ca samādhismiṁ gocarakusalo.

sn34.6:1.7.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo ca hoti, samādhismiṁ gocarakusalo ca.

sn34.6:1.8.pli: Tatra, bhikkhave, yvāyaṁ jhāyī samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo ca hoti samādhismiṁ gocarakusalo ca ayaṁ imesaṁ catunnaṁ jhāyīnaṁ aggo ca seṭṭho ca mokkho ca uttamo ca pavaro ca.

sn34.7:0.3.eng: Projecting the Mind Purified by Immersion

sn34.7:1.4.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo hoti, na samādhismiṁ abhinīhārakusalo.
sn34.7:1.4.eng: One meditator is skilled in immersion but not in projecting the mind purified by immersion. …”
sn34.7:1.4.comm: They cannot “project” the mind to the various psychic powers and higher knowledges (DN 2:84.6).

sn34.7:1.5.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ abhinīhārakusalo hoti, na samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo.

sn34.7:1.6.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī neva samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo hoti, na ca samādhismiṁ abhinīhārakusalo.

sn34.7:1.7.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo ca hoti, samādhismiṁ abhinīhārakusalo ca.

sn34.7:1.8.pli: Tatra, bhikkhave, yvāyaṁ jhāyī samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo ca hoti samādhismiṁ abhinīhārakusalo ca ayaṁ imesaṁ catunnaṁ jhāyīnaṁ aggo ca seṭṭho ca mokkho ca uttamo ca pavaro ca.

sn34.8:1.4.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo hoti, na samādhismiṁ sakkaccakārī.
sn34.8:1.4.eng: One meditator is skilled in immersion but not in practicing carefully for it. …”
sn34.8:1.4.comm: They cannot maintain a systematic practice throughout the day (AN 3.19:2.3).

sn34.8:1.5.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ sakkaccakārī hoti, na samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo.

sn34.8:1.6.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī neva samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo hoti, na ca samādhismiṁ sakkaccakārī.

sn34.8:1.7.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo ca hoti, samādhismiṁ sakkaccakārī ca.

sn34.8:1.8.pli: Tatra, bhikkhave, yvāyaṁ jhāyī samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo ca hoti samādhismiṁ sakkaccakārī ca ayaṁ imesaṁ catunnaṁ jhāyīnaṁ aggo ca seṭṭho ca mokkho ca uttamo ca pavaro ca.

sn34.9:1.4.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo hoti, na samādhismiṁ sātaccakārī.
sn34.9:1.4.eng: One meditator is skilled in immersion but not in practicing persistently for it. …”
sn34.9:1.4.comm: The failure of “persistence” in a meditator is often linked with their emotional hard-heartedness and mental shackles (MN 16:3.1). Without a faithful, kind, and wholesome disposition it is difficult to sustain a meditation practice.

sn34.9:1.5.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ sātaccakārī hoti, na samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo.

sn34.9:1.6.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī neva samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo hoti, na ca samādhismiṁ sātaccakārī.

sn34.9:1.7.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo ca hoti, samādhismiṁ sātaccakārī ca.

sn34.9:1.8.pli: Tatra, bhikkhave, yvāyaṁ jhāyī samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo ca hoti, samādhismiṁ sātaccakārī ca ayaṁ imesaṁ catunnaṁ jhāyīnaṁ aggo ca seṭṭho ca mokkho ca uttamo ca pavaro ca.
